Cystic acne is deep, painful, and doesn’t go away easily . What helps: • Benzoyl peroxide cleanser – reduces bacteria • Adapalene (retinoid) – controls oil and inflammation • Oral treatment – for severe or persistent acne How to use: Use benzoyl peroxide cleanser (leave 1–2 min, then rinse) . The COMPLETE protocol to safely erase Cystic Acne (Blind Pimples). 💣🆘 Squeezing a deep, cystic blind pimple (one with no whitehead) is the fastest way to cause a permanent ice-pick scar or hyperpigmentation. You need a targeted, multi-step attack to draw it out safely. 🧬 Here is the exact 5-step squad to fix it in order: 🔥 STEP 1 (WARM COMPRESS): Softens the hard tissue and draws the deep infection closer to the surface barrier. 💎 STEP 2 (MICRO-DARTS): The delivery system. Tiny, dissolvable darts create painless microscopic channels past the tough epidermis. 💚 STEP 3 (SALICYLIC ACID): The neutralizer. Travels down the new channels to dissolve the oil and fight the infection at the core. 🧽 STEP 4 (HYDROCOLLOID): The extractor. Once the infection is broken down, this patch safely absorbs the fluid to flatten the swelling. 💙 STEP 5 (CENTELLA/CICA): The peacekeeper. Cools down the angry post-blemish redness to prevent dark spots from forming. 📌 SAVE this exact order for your next skincare emergency and FOLLOW for more 3D skin science! 🐼 #cysticacne #blindpimple #acnetreatment #pimplepatch #salicylicacid

this was my skin before… acne, dark spots, rough texture, everything 😭 and if you’re dealing with this right now, I get it. it’s frustrating when nothing seems to work but here’s what actually helped me clear my skin (and what can help you too): first, understand your skin… not every breakout is the same. acne, fungal acne, irritation, damaged barrier—they all need different treatments second, stop overloading your skin using too many actives at once will only make things worse. keep your routine simple and intentional third, focus on these key ingredients: • salicylic acid – clears pores and reduces breakouts • niacinamide – controls oil + helps with dark spots • azelaic acid / tranexamic acid – fades hyperpigmentation • retinol (at night) – improves texture over time fourth, repair your skin barrier hydration is not optional. use gentle cleansers, moisturizers, and avoid harsh scrubs fifth, sunscreen every single day this is what protects your progress. without it, dark spots will keep coming back and lastly… be consistent clear skin is not overnight. it took time, patience, and understanding my skin if your skin looks like my “before”, just know it can get better save this as a reminder to stay consistent 🤍 #acnetransformation #clearskinjourney #hyperpigmentation #skincareinlagos #skincaretips

If your acne is hormonal, drying it out isn’t the answer. Here are treatments that actually target hormonal acne: 1. Oral spironolactone Helps block androgen signaling from stimulating oil glands, which can reduce hormonal breakouts over time. 2. Topical spironolactone Works similarly, but applied directly to the skin to target oil production locally. 3. Winlevi (clascoterone) An FDA-approved topical anti-androgen that blocks hormones at the oil gland. 4. Isotretinoin (Accutane) The closest thing we have to long-term remission because it actually shrinks the oil glands, so the hormonal signal has much less “target” to act on. At Honeydew, the goal isn’t just to dry out your skin - it’s to treat the root cause and give you the best chance at lasting control. #hormonalacne #hormoneimbalance #spironolactone #accutane #dermpa

PSA: Hibiclens is NOT daily skincare. 🚨 Hibiclens (chlorhexidine) is a surgical antiseptic cleanser used in medical settings before procedures. It’s not meant to be used as your everyday face or body wash. Daily use can: • Disrupt your skin barrier • Alter your skin microbiome • Cause dryness & irritation The exception? Certain medical conditions like hidradenitis suppurativa, where doctors may recommend it. Bottom line: A cleanser that kills bacteria ≠ a healthy daily skincare routine. #skincareeducation #hibiclens #chlorhexidine #skintips #bodyodor #dermtok #hidradenitissuppurativa

Sunburn peeling might look satisfying, but it is actually your body removing skin cells that were damaged by ultraviolet radiation. When UV light injures the DNA inside skin cells, those cells can trigger a built in self destruct process called apoptosis. Your immune system then clears them away, which is why the top layer of skin starts peeling a few days after a bad sunburn. It is essentially your body getting rid of cells that were too genetically damaged to keep around. #sunburn #skincancerprevention #dermatology #medicaleducation

Retinol burn occurs when the skin becomes irritated due to overuse or improper application of retinol-based products for your skin type. Using high concentrations of retinol or applying it too frequently can also increase the risk.  To alleviate symptoms: allow your skin to heal by temporarily stopping the use of retinol, use a hydrating moisturizer, avoid harsh products that can further irritate the skin, protect your skin from sun exposure as retinol increases sun sensitivity.  @lisat449  #dermatologist #doctor #retinolburn #skincare #skintok #retinol #antiaging #dermreacts

I want to be completely clear: My results have come from six years of using retinoids and three years of using azelaic acid. Most of my before and after pictures show the power of consistent, long terms use of retinoids.  That being said, my skin really levelled up when I started using azelaic acid. I didn’t even start using it because I wanted more results, I was perfectly happy with my results. But I developed perioral dermatitis and azelaic acid is a treatment for that. My skin would certainly still have its glass quality without it, but azelaic acid is very soothing - something a retinoid absolutely isn’t! So when I started getting little red splotches and flareups, yes the retinoid can help, but it has nothing on azelaic acid. What you see in the first clip is irritation on holiday, and the subsequent clips are my skin on a more typical basis. Those types of flareups are non-existent for me now, and I do put most of that down to azelaic acid. I think your retinoid should act as your anti-acne, skin resurfacing an anti-aging heavy lifter. The AA helps with flareups, hyperpigmentation and evening out your skin tone. I put all my recommended products on my profile x #azelaicacid #skincare

Here’s some more info on the Skin 1004 Centella Ampoule. When it comes to extracts, the straight plant material is called centella asiatica extract but so is a pre-made formula that comes from any raw material supplier.  Which means, with this serum, you are getting 100% of a pre made raw material formula that likely contains less than 5% of the actual centella asiatica plant.  But this brand doesn’t tell you how much of the actual plant is in this formula. Nor do they tell you the extraction process, what part of the plant, or whether it is standardised in any of the active molecules that make centella so beneficial to your skin.  I know a centella supplier that tests for these molecules and they have to reach 95% purity or the batch is rejected. Even 94.9% won’t make it through. So you know you’re getting these active components that actually impact your skin.  If skin 1004 purchases this complex from a raw material supplier, they aren’t doing no R&D, no formulating, no innovation, just repackaging something that already exists, slapping 100% on it and selling it to you for a crazy mark up.  Surely this is illegal right?  Well in Australia it would be which is why I called it deceptive and shady. In Australia, if you get this from a raw material supplier and put it in your skincare, you need to list most of the ingredients in order of quantity.  But in Korea you only have to list the name of the raw material complex.   If this works for you and you like it, keep using it. You don’t actually need a lot of centella to see results.  But apparently these Korean regulations are meant to be changing soon. #kbeautyskincare #beautyhacks #koreanskincare #kbeauty #skin1004  #centella

Spironolactone works for acne because it lowers androgen activity. The real question is… why do so many women need that now? It’s not that we all suddenly developed “acne genes.” It’s that modern physiology is constantly pushing androgen signaling up — even when labs look normal. Chronic stress Blood sugar chaos Gut and liver overload A nervous system stuck in survival mode All of these amplify oil production and inflammation at the skin. So when you take spironolactone, oil goes down and breakouts calm. That doesn’t mean it fixed the root cause — it means the signal got muted. And for many women, the moment they stop? The acne comes right back. If the goal is lasting clear skin, you have to address why androgen signaling was loud in the first place. That’s the part no one explains. ✨ and this is where a root cause approach to acne can change everything! #spironolactone #acne #acnehealing

It really does matter how you take your Blackseed oil. Depending on your needs, this is going to differ. Every individual is different so it’s hard to say exactly what’s going to help someone the most so keep in mind that these are just some suggestions for dosages and timing. 1.) For High Blood Sugar or High Blood Pressure, it’s best to split the dose day and night to keep it steady in the system because blood sugar and blood pressure fluctuate throughout the day and the half life of thymoquinone plasma is about 4 to 6 hours depending. 2.) if you have auto immune conditions or have chronic inflammation, it is best to take it once daily about a half a teaspoon to 1 teaspoon per day— nighttime tends to be better for joint pain and inflammation as many inflammatory cytokines can spike at night. Taking it with food helps to make it more gentle on the system. 3.) if you have gut sensitivity or reflux making sure to take it with a little bit of food that has fat maybe a little bit of protein is the best approach so that it buffers the system and doesn’t hit so strongly. This can help you avoid the burping as well. Starting off with a lower dosage like a quarter of a teaspoon to a half a teaspoon once a day is good for starters. If you’re having an active flare, it might be best to avoid it until the flare calms consistency is also key. 4.)  for histamine issues or allergies once per day in the morning is most likely going to be best since histamines can be higher in the morning. It also covers you for your most reactive hours. It helps mass cells behave better so being consistent is ideal. You can split your dosage if you have postnasal drip or allergy issues at night as well. 5.) for perimenopausal or menopausal symptoms once per day is ideal during the day is best. If you have blood sugar crashes or if your energy is low and you have daytime inflammation it’s best to take it with a little bit of protein and fat during the day— however if your symptoms worsen at night, you can split your doses a quarter of a teaspoon to a half a teaspoon with a little bit of food. It is best to consult your doctor before using any herbal supplements, especially if you are pregnant, breast-feeding, or on medication. It is advised to take a break with Blackseed oil after three months since there aren’t any long-term studies to support longer-term use. Cycling offer for a few weeks and then back on is ideal This is not medical advice, and I am not a medical professional.

Stop trying to pop those tiny, itchy bumps! 🛑🍄 If you have clusters of tiny, uniform bumps on your chest, back, or forehead that itch and NEVER come to a whitehead... that is NOT normal acne. It is Fungal Acne (Malassezia Folliculitis). 🧬 Normal pimple creams will not work. In fact, heavy oils and thick creams actually feed the yeast and make it spread! You need a completely different approach. Here is your dermatologist-approved Fungal Rescue Squad: 🧼 STEP 1 (The Eradicator): Wash the area with an anti-fungal ingredient like Ketoconazole or Zinc Pyrithione (yes, the exact same stuff in anti-dandruff shampoo!) to stop the yeast from multiplying. 🧪 STEP 2 (The Unclogger): Salicylic Acid (BHA) is oil-soluble, meaning it dives into the infected follicle to melt away the trapped dead skin cells flattening the bumps. 💧 STEP 3 (The Safe Plump): Fungi feed on lipids (oils). You must repair your skin barrier using a 100% oil-free water gel hydrator so you don't accidentally feed the yeast again! 📌 SAVE this safe extraction routine, FOLLOW @skincaretheorydaily, and comment GUIDE to get our ultimate step-by-step Skincare Playbook sent right to your DMs! 🐼✨ #fungalacne #chestacne #tinybumps #texturedskin #skincarehacks

How to kill fungal acne for good! Ketoconazole is an antifungal medication commonly used to treat “fungal acne,” which is actually a condition called Malassezia folliculitis. This condition is caused by an overgrowth of yeast (Malassezia) on the skin rather than bacteria. Ketoconazole works by disrupting the fungal cell membrane, specifically, it inhibits the production of ergosterol, a key component the fungus needs to survive. Without ergosterol, the fungal cells become weak, leaky, and eventually die, reducing the overgrowth that leads to itchy, acne-like bumps. In terms of use, ketoconazole is often applied topically in the form of creams or shampoos (like 1–2% formulations). Even though it’s marketed as a dandruff treatment, the shampoo is frequently used on areas like the forehead, chest, or back! Step by step: you apply it to the affected skin, leave it on for about 3–5 minutes, then rinse, usually a few times per week. Consistency is key, as it helps keep the yeast population under control and prevents flare-ups. #fungalacne#ketaconazole#skincare#cura

Spending $40 on a body acne wash is criminal when this exists 👇 Body acne washes are a total racket. $30, $40, $50 for something you can make at home for $2.50 and it’ll actually be gentler on sensitive skin. Here’s why this works: salicylic acid unclogs pores without stripping your skin dry. The trick is diluting it into a creamy, glycerin-rich base so you get the treatment without the irritation. Here’s the exact recipe: 	∙	Dollar Tree 3% salicylic acid cream (full tube) 	∙	Simple baby shampoo (pour out until you have about 60g left) 	∙	Mix them together That’s it. You end up with a 1% salicylic acid creamy body wash for $2.50 that does exactly what the expensive stuff promises. Your skin doesn’t know the difference. Your wallet does. 💊 #philsmypharmacist #dollartreehacks #healtheducation #acnebodywash #bodyacne

And if this is happening to you, you don't have to “just deal with it.” Birth control and acne medications like spironolactone can be great but Dr. Rubin shares that there can be side effects that you need to be aware of. Part 1 of Rating azelaic acid products that I’ve tried! #azelaicacid #azelaicacidserum #koreanskincare #koreanskincareproducts #skincareviral

Azelaic acid YES or NO?  9 must-know facts. 🟢 Acne — YES (antibacterial + anti-inflammatory + helps unclog pores) 🟢 Dark spots — YES (helps with PIH + melasma by targeting excess melanin) 🔴 Wrinkles — NO (retinol is better for that) 🟢 Rosacea — YES (one of the few well-studied options) 🟢 Daily use — YES (start 1x/day → build up to 2x/day if tolerated) 🟡 Stinging at first — SOMETIMES (usually fades in minutes) 🟢 Purging — UNLIKELY (generally gentler than retinoids/BHA) 🟢 Sensitive skin — YES, with caution (introduce slowly) ✅ Would I recommend it? Absolutely. Don’t layer it with retinoids or BHA at the same time—alternate and let your skin adjust first. Pregnancy-safe. Let's talk about salicylic acid wash and how it can improve your skin! Salicylic acid is a type of beta hydroxy acid (BHA) that's commonly used in skincare products. It's known for its ability to exfoliate and unclog pores, making it a great ingredient for those with oily and acne-prone skin. Using a salicylic acid wash can help improve the appearance of your skin in a number of ways. It can: Clear away dead skin cells and unclog pores, which can reduce the appearance of blackheads and whiteheads. Reduce inflammation and redness, which can make pimples less noticeable and  elp control oil production, which can prevent future breakouts. Don't forget to follow up with a moisturizer to keep your skin hydrated!  So, if you're struggling with acne or oily skin, consider incorporating a salicylic acid wash into your routine.
